FieldGuard / LBS INT
Release Surfaces Spec Source GSMmap Download ↓
v1.1.0 · Confidential issue · LBS INT

A pocket instrument for the targeted.

FieldGuard sits one layer beneath the operating system — on the radio modem, in the kernel network stack, against the raw flash — and reports what the carrier is doing to the device in your pocket. Silent SMS. SIM‑OTA writes. Pegasus stage‑one. SS7 location pulls.

Version 1.1.0 adds Secure Comms — end-to-end PGP encrypted group messaging for the field team. Ed25519‑signed, X25519‑encrypted, key generated locally and never transmitted. Gated to @lbs-int.com accounts only.

Get FieldGuard 1.1.0 What it watches
Signed builds Open source Station online PGP E2E comms
22:14
FieldGuard · Live
Station OK
SS7 · ATI+02 12:14:06
AnyTimeInterrogation from foreign GT — blocked.
SMS · Type-0+02 12:13:51
Silent ping PDU — PID 0x40 / DCS 0x18.
OTA · SIM+02 12:13:22
SIM-OTA write attempt on STK applet — logged.
SIG · DB+02 12:12:48
Signature pull from station — 1,284 patterns.
NET · Pegasus+02 12:12:09
DNS hit on watch-listed C2 — flagged.
Secure Comms
E2E Encrypted
PGP · Ed25519 · group:OPS-NORTH
m.larsen · verified
Device shows SS7 ATI from GT 45009. Confirm?
you · signed
Confirmed. MAP timestamp matches the log. Escalating.
j.nielsen · verified
IMSI capture active on that cell. Moving target.
you · signed
Copy. Switching to backup. Hold comms.
Encrypt & send…
Live
PDU
SS7
Probe
Comms
New in 1.1.0
Secure Comms — open-registration E2EE messaging. Unbreakable on any classical computer.
Group channels and private 1:1 DMs open to any registered user. Every message is OpenPGP-encrypted and Ed25519-signed on device before a single byte touches the wire. The relay server never sees plaintext. LBS INT staff get a cryptographically verified member badge. Auto-update keeps every installation current the moment a new signed release ships.
Ed25519 signing X25519 ECDH AES-256 payload 1:1 Direct Messages Offline capable Verified senders Open registration Key never leaves device Auto-update notify
§ Release

Current build — signed and shipping.

Signed by LBS INT, mirrored on this host, served over TLS. v1.1.0 introduces Secure Comms alongside all existing detection and PDU tooling.

FieldGuard
v1.1.0
Android · ZIP →
For the device under attack
v1.1.0 · Secure Comms · SS7 · Pegasus · SIM-OTA · API 26+
sha256 · 5c0d9f22…14b1c
PC live view · PIN →
Pair browser with the Android app
Phone: PC Bridge → Create Session · then open this link
relay · same host as APK mirror
What's new in v1.1.0
Secure Comms — group channels
OpenPGP E2E encrypted group messaging. Ed25519 signing + X25519 ECDH. Private key generated on device, never transmitted to the relay. LBS-internal, public, and private group types.
1:1 Direct Messages
Encrypted DMs between any two registered users. Each message is sealed to both parties so either can decrypt their own history. Peer key status shown in real time.
Open registration
Any valid email can join Secure Comms. @lbs-int.com addresses are automatically granted a verified LBS member badge. User search returns display names only — email addresses are never exposed.
Auto-update notifications
App checks our signed release mirror on launch and foreground. When a new build ships, a native Android notification appears in the shade — tap to download the signed APK directly. Cache prevents unnecessary round-trips.
SCCP LUDT transport
SS7 MMS payloads now automatically promote to Long Unitdata (Q.713 §3.11) when TCAP exceeds 255 bytes. MO-ForwardSM delivery fixed.
Distributed via the LBS INT release channel. All releases Release notes
§ 01 · Surfaces

Seven instruments, hooked at the lowest accessible layer.

None of them rely on the operating system noticing first. Detection happens on the radio, in the kernel network stack, against the raw flash — before user‑space ever sees the event. v1.1.0 adds a dedicated encrypted comms plane.

Radio Layer Pickup

PID 0x7F · IEI 0x70/71

Inbound SMS PDUs read straight from the RIL before any other process is notified. Type-0 silent, SIM OTA, STK ProactiveCommand, Class-0 flash, binary — classified, dumped, archived.

Network Capture

VPN-TUN · Android

Full packet capture against a watch-list of NSO/Pegasus C2 endpoints, SIGTRAN traffic toward the device, GTP tunnel creation and ICMP-redirect attempts.

Filesystem Scanner

Stage-1 · RAT

Byte-pattern sweep over storage. SS7 RAT payloads, Pegasus stage-one shells, SIM OTA scripts, generic implant signatures. Database refreshes from the station on its own schedule.

SS7 Catalogue

15+ classes

Reference library of attack PDU shapes — classification by PID/DCS/UDH, plain language descriptions, mitigation pointers. Pulled inline as detections fire.

PDU Workbench

SMS-SUBMIT

Build silent, OTA, STK, binary and WAP-Push PDUs from templates. Hex dump aligned with decoded fields. For verification — not for mischief.

Station Link

TCP 5556

Persistent channel back to the LBS operations station. Pulls signatures, pushes alerts, relays telemetry. Drops to local-only mode on its own when offline.

New 1.1.0

Secure Comms

PGP · Ed25519 · X25519 · DMs

Group channels and 1:1 DMs open to any registered user. OpenPGP encrypted and Ed25519-signed on device before any byte reaches the relay. Zero-knowledge server — plaintext is never stored or transmitted unencrypted. LBS INT staff carry a cryptographically verified member badge.

§ 02 · Spec

What it needs — modest.

The radio layer does the heavy lifting; FieldGuard is the lens. Secure Comms requires a relay connection on first login only — all subsequent communication is encrypted client-side.

—Android (handset)PC / tablet (browser)
RoleRIL capture · alerts · PDU tools · Secure CommsLive event stream via PIN pairing
OSAndroid 8.0 · API 26+Current Chrome / Firefox / Edge / Safari
Architecturearm64-v8a · armeabi-v7aAny (TLS 1.2+)
PermissionsSMS · READ_PHONE_STATE · VPNNone beyond the web page
Footprint~80 MB on diskNo install · open viewer URL
NetworkOptional · station check-inHTTPS to this host only
Comms cryptoOpenPGP v4 · Ed25519 signing · X25519 ECDH · AES-256-GCM payload · key local onlyN/A
Comms accessOpen registration (any email) · @lbs-int.com auto-verified · keypair generated on first login, never transmittedN/A
Auto-updateChecks signed release endpoint on launch + foreground · native notification · 1 hr cache · direct APK downloadN/A
§ 03 · Source

What's inside — nothing hidden.

React Native shell. Kotlin and C++ for the parts that touch the radio. Secure Comms built entirely in TypeScript using OpenPGP.js with no server-side key handling.

Clone

# public source tree
git clone https://github.com/LBSINTER/LBS-FieldGuard.git

# or browse the on-host mirror
https://fieldguard.lbs-int.com/source/

Map

  • Android shellandroid/ · Kotlin · React Native bridge
  • Native modulesdesktop-bridge/ · C++ packet plane
  • UI & logicsrc/ · TypeScript
  • Secure Commssrc/comms/ · PGP · store · API · screens
  • Test surface__tests__/ · Jest
§ 04 · Encryption

Offline end-to-end. Unbreakable by any classical adversary.

Every Secure Comms message is sealed before it leaves the device. The relay is a dumb pipe that moves opaque ciphertext. There is no backdoor, no master key, no server-side decryption — by architecture, not by policy.

Encryption layer
AES-256-GCM payload
Every message body is symmetrically encrypted with a 256-bit session key derived via X25519 ECDH. NIST-vetted. The ciphertext is what travels — plaintext never does.
Key exchange
X25519 Elliptic Diffie-Hellman
Curve25519 ECDH. No message is encrypted to the server. Multi-recipient: DMs are sealed to both sender and recipient so each party can decrypt their own history — nothing else.
Signatures
Ed25519 per-message signing
Every outbound message is signed with the sender's Ed25519 private key. Recipients verify against the sender's published public key. A tampered or spoofed message fails verification — visibly.
Key custody
Zero-knowledge server
Private keys are generated on the device and stored encrypted by the user's passphrase. The relay holds only the public key for distribution. No HSM, no escrow, no recovery — by design.
Compliance alignment
GDPR · NIS2 · ISO 27001
Architecture satisfies GDPR Art. 25 (data protection by design), NIS2 Art. 21 (appropriate technical security measures), and the cryptographic controls described in ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A.8.24.
Auto-update
Signed release delivery
On every launch and foreground, the app checks fieldguard.lbs-int.com/api.php/app/version for a newer signed build. A native notification appears in the Android shade. One tap — download and install. No Play Store dependency, no third-party delivery chain.
Statement of assurance
LBS INT (CVR 41439874, Danish PMV) operates FieldGuard as part of its GSMmap programme. The Secure Comms relay is operated from EU-domiciled infrastructure. No message content is accessible to LBS INT or any third party. Source is available at github.com/LBSINTER/LBS-FieldGuard for independent audit. We keep pace with cryptographic standards — when the field moves, we move.
Part of the GSMmap programme
FieldGuard ships the same signalling intelligence that powers gsmmap.com — the global map of carrier MAP / SS7 exposure — directly into the device under attack.
Open GSMmap →
